org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders

Class AnalogSwerveEncoder

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders.AnalogSwerveEncoder
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    SwerveEncoder

    public class AnalogSwerveEncoder
extends java.lang.Object
implements SwerveEncoder
    SRS Hub analog swerve encoder implementation.

    This class uses the SRS Hub's analog inputs to read swerve module encoders. It supports analog potentiometers and magnetic encoders that output a voltage proportional to position.

    Supported Encoder Types:

    • Analog Potentiometers: 0-3.3V output, typically 270° or 360° range
    • Magnetic Encoders: AS5048B, MA730, etc. with analog output
    • East Loop Components: Any analog encoder with voltage output

    Analog Configuration:

    SRS Hub analog inputs:

    • Voltage Range: 0-3.3V (mapped to 0.0-1.0 normalized)
    • Resolution: 12-bit ADC (4096 levels)
    • Pins: 1-12 available

    Calibration:

    Analog encoders require calibration to map voltage to angle:

     // Single-turn potentiometer (270° range)
 AnalogSwerveEncoder encoder = new AnalogSwerveEncoder(
     srsHub, pin,
     0.0,           // minVoltage (0V)
     3.3,           // maxVoltage (3.3V)
     -Math.PI,      // minAngle (-180° in radians)
     Math.PI,       // maxAngle (+180° in radians)
     0.0            // offset (calibrated so forward = 0)
 );

    Performance vs Digital:

    • Pros: Lower cost, simpler wiring, no I2C congestion
    • Cons: Lower resolution (4096 vs 8192), susceptible to noise, drift over time
    • Best For: Low-budget builds, testing, or when OctoQuad ports are full
    See Also:
    SwerveEncoder, SRSHub, OctoQuadSwerveEncoder

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private double cachedPositionRadians
      Cached position reading (radians).
      private double cachedVelocityRadiansPerSec
      Cached velocity reading (radians/second).
      private boolean isSteeringEncoder
      Whether this encoder is a steering encoder (true) or drive encoder (false).
      private double maxAngle
      Maximum angle in radians corresponding to maxVoltage.
      private double maxVoltage
      Maximum voltage corresponding to maxAngle (volts).
      private double minAngle
      Minimum angle in radians corresponding to minVoltage.
      private double minVoltage
      Minimum voltage corresponding to minAngle (volts).
      private double offsetRadians
      Angle offset to calibrate zero position (radians).
      private int pin
      Analog pin number on the SRS Hub (1-12).
      private double previousPositionRadians
      Previous position for velocity calculation.
      private long previousTimestampNanos
      Previous timestamp for velocity calculation (nanoseconds).
      private SRSHub srsHub
      SRS Hub instance for analog readings.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub, int pin)
      Creates an analog drive encoder.
      AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub, int pin, double minAngle, double maxAngle, double offsetRadians)
      Creates an analog steering encoder.
      AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub, int pin, double minVoltage, double maxVoltage, double minAngle, double maxAngle, double offsetRadians)
      Creates an analog steering encoder with custom voltage range.

    • Field Detail

      • srsHub

        private final SRSHub srsHub
        SRS Hub instance for analog readings.

      • pin

        private final int pin
        Analog pin number on the SRS Hub (1-12).

      • minVoltage

        private final double minVoltage
        Minimum voltage corresponding to minAngle (volts).

      • maxVoltage

        private final double maxVoltage
        Maximum voltage corresponding to maxAngle (volts).

      • minAngle

        private final double minAngle
        Minimum angle in radians corresponding to minVoltage.

      • maxAngle

        private final double maxAngle
        Maximum angle in radians corresponding to maxVoltage.

      • offsetRadians

        private final double offsetRadians
        Angle offset to calibrate zero position (radians). Subtracted from the reading to set forward = 0.

      • isSteeringEncoder

        private final boolean isSteeringEncoder
        Whether this encoder is a steering encoder (true) or drive encoder (false).

      • cachedPositionRadians

        private double cachedPositionRadians
        Cached position reading (radians).

      • cachedVelocityRadiansPerSec

        private double cachedVelocityRadiansPerSec
        Cached velocity reading (radians/second).

      • previousPositionRadians

        private double previousPositionRadians
        Previous position for velocity calculation.

      • previousTimestampNanos

        private long previousTimestampNanos
        Previous timestamp for velocity calculation (nanoseconds).

    • Constructor Detail

      • AnalogSwerveEncoder

        public AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub,
                           int pin,
                           double minAngle,
                           double maxAngle,
                           double offsetRadians)
        Creates an analog steering encoder.

        This constructor assumes the full voltage range (0-3.3V) maps to the full angle range.

        Parameters:
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance
        pin - analog pin number (1-12)
        minAngle - minimum angle in radians corresponding to 0V
        maxAngle - maximum angle in radians corresponding to 3.3V
        offsetRadians - angle offset to calibrate zero position (radians)

      • AnalogSwerveEncoder

        public AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub,
                           int pin,
                           double minVoltage,
                           double maxVoltage,
                           double minAngle,
                           double maxAngle,
                           double offsetRadians)
        Creates an analog steering encoder with custom voltage range.

        Use this if your encoder doesn't use the full 0-3.3V range.

        Parameters:
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance
        pin - analog pin number (1-12)
        minVoltage - minimum voltage (volts, typically 0.0)
        maxVoltage - maximum voltage (volts, typically 3.3)
        minAngle - minimum angle in radians corresponding to minVoltage
        maxAngle - maximum angle in radians corresponding to maxVoltage
        offsetRadians - angle offset to calibrate zero position (radians)

      • AnalogSwerveEncoder

        public AnalogSwerveEncoder(SRSHub srsHub,
                           int pin)
        Creates an analog drive encoder.

        Note: Analog encoders are not recommended for drive wheels (velocity measurement). Use OctoQuad digital encoders for drive wheels instead. This implementation returns velocity calculated from position changes, which is less accurate than quadrature.

        Parameters:
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance
        pin - analog pin number (1-12)

    • Method Detail

      • getPosition

        public double getPosition()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Reads the current position from the encoder.

        For steering encoders, this returns the absolute angle in radians.

        For drive encoders, this returns position in ticks (if supported).

        Specified by:
        getPosition in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        position in appropriate units (radians for steering, ticks for drive)

      • getVelocity

        public double getVelocity()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Reads the current velocity from the encoder.

        For drive encoders, this returns velocity in ticks per second.

        For steering encoders, this may return 0 if velocity is not available.

        Specified by:
        getVelocity in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        velocity in appropriate units (typically ticks/second)

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Resets the encoder to zero.

        Not all encoders support this (absolute encoders cannot be reset).

        Specified by:
        reset in interface SwerveEncoder

      • getEncoderType

        public java.lang.String getEncoderType()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Gets the encoder type for telemetry/debugging.
        Specified by:
        getEncoderType in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        encoder type name (e.g., "OctoQuad", "Analog")

      • getCachedPosition

        public double getCachedPosition()
        Gets the cached position reading (radians).

        Useful for telemetry to avoid redundant analog reads.

        Returns:
        cached position in radians

      • getCachedVelocity

        public double getCachedVelocity()
        Gets the cached velocity reading (radians/second).
        Returns:
        cached velocity in radians/second
